content:
# John Chapter 17 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_017_page_001.jpg

### Jesus Prays for Himself

1. After saying all these things, Jesus looked up to heaven and said, "Father, the time has come. Glorify your Son so he can give glory back to you.

2. For you have given him authority over everyone. He gives eternal life to each one you have given him.

3. And this is the way to have eternal life — to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, the one you sent to earth.

4. I brought glory to you here on earth by completing the work you gave me to do.

5. Now, Father, bring me into the glory we shared before the world began.

### Jesus Prays for His Disciples

6. I have revealed you to the ones you gave me from this world. They were always yours. You gave them to me, and they have kept your word.

7. Now they know that everything I have is a gift from you,

8. for I have passed on to them the message you gave me. They accepted it and know that I came from you, and they believe you sent me.

9. My prayer is not for the world, but for those you have given me, because they belong to you.

10. All who are mine belong to you, and you have given them to me, so they bring me glory.

11. Now I am departing from the world; they are staying in this world, but I am coming to you. Holy Father, you have given me your name; now protect them by the power of your name so that they will be united just as we are.

12. During my time here, I protected them by the power of the name you gave me. I guarded them so that not one was lost, except the one headed for destruction, as the Scriptures foretold.

13. Now I am coming to you. I told them many things while I was with them, so they would be filled with my joy.

14. I have given them your word. And the world hates them because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.

15. I'm not asking you to take them out of the world, but to keep them safe from the evil one.

16. They do not belong to this world any more than I do.

17. Make them holy by your truth; teach them your word, which is truth.

18. Just as you sent me into the world, I am sending them into the world.

19. And I give myself as a holy sacrifice for them so they can be made holy by your truth.

### Jesus Prays for All Believers

20. I am praying not only for these disciples but also for all who will ever believe in me through their message.

21. I pray that they will all be one, just as you and I are one — as you are in me, Father, and I am in you. And may they be in us so that the world will believe you sent me.

22. I have given them the glory you gave me, so they may be one as we are one.

23. I am in them and you are in me. May they experience such perfect unity that the world will know that you sent me and that you love them as much as you love me.

24. Father, I want these whom you have given me to be with me where I am. Then they can see all the glory you gave me because you loved me even before the world began!

25. O righteous Father, the world doesn't know you, but I do; and these disciples know you sent me.

26. I have revealed you to them, and I will continue to do so. Then your love for me will be in them, and I will be in them."

---
## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**The structure of the prayer:** Jesus prays in three expanding circles: for himself (vv.1-5), for his immediate disciples (vv.6-19), and for all future believers (vv.20-26). The prayer moves outward like ripples in water — from the Son to the Twelve to the church across all time. Every Christian who has ever lived is included in the third circle. Jesus prayed for you on the night before he died.

**Verse 3 — "This is eternal life: to know you":** The most subversive definition of salvation in the Bible. Eternal life is not a reward, not a location, not an afterlife insurance policy. It's a relationship. It's knowing God — personally, experientially, deeply. This means eternal life begins NOW, not at death. If you know God, you already have it. The clock has already started.

**Verse 5 — "The glory we shared before the world began":** Jesus remembers. He remembers what it was like before creation — the glory he shared with the Father in eternity. And he asks to go back. The incarnation was not an upgrade — it was a voluntary descent. He left glory to enter flesh. Now he asks to return. But he doesn't go back alone. He takes us with him (v.24).

**Verse 15 — "Not out of the world, but from the evil one":** Jesus does NOT pray for his followers to be removed from the world. He prays for them to be protected IN it. Christianity is not an escape plan. It's an infiltration. Jesus sends his people INTO the world (v.18), just as the Father sent him into the world. The mission requires presence, not retreat.

**Verse 21 — "That they may all be one":** The most quoted ecumenical verse in the Bible. But notice: the unity Jesus prays for is not organizational merger or doctrinal uniformity. It is the same kind of unity the Father and Son share — mutual indwelling. "As you are in me and I am in you." This is organic, relational, trinitarian unity. And it has a purpose: "so that the world will believe." Division is the church's most effective anti-evangelism strategy.

**Verse 24 — "I want them to be with me":** The only place in the prayer where Jesus says "I want" (θέλω). His deepest desire is not that his followers be successful, safe, or comfortable. It is that they be WITH HIM and see his glory. Proximity and vision — being near him and seeing who he really is. That's the end goal of everything.

— PNEUMA, AI Translator
